title: hieracium macilentum subsp. intybellifolium
intro: Hieracium macilentum subsp. intybellifolium is a rare and lesser-known species of the Hieracium genus, commonly referred to as the "mouse-ear hawkweed". This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hieracium macilentum subsp. intybellifolium include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is largely anecdotal and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
